hero.badge: Engineering Lead · Software Architect · FATEC ADS
hero.description: 15+ years in IT and infrastructure. Modern fullstack with architecture-first approach.
hero.highlight: Those who understand the system drive the AI.
hero.subtitle: Those who only know syntax compete with it.

manifesto.quote: I won't be the dev who grabs the biggest feature. I'll be the one who ensures everyone's features work together — on standard, with quality, production-ready.
manifesto.author: Contribution Plan · March 2025

diocese.title: Diocese SaaS Platform
diocese.description: Turborepo monorepo. RBAC Diocese→Chapel→Editor. Multi-tenancy with RLS and per-tenant Feature Flags. Own stack without external CMS dependency.

capelas.title: Chapel Management
capelas.description: 1 admin + 200 branches with editable layouts. Headless Strapi architecture. Scaling limits motivated a complete refactor to own stack.

waas.title: Website as a Service
waas.description: 1 deploy + N clients. Config-driven engine with Registry Pattern, dynamic renderer and theme engine via CSS variables. Multi-tenant by host.

market.title: C4ts Market Research
market.description: Platform for freelance client acquisition and local positioning. Forms and blob for quote requests and product briefs.

architecture.sectionLabel: // architecture — decisions that allow swapping technology without rewriting the system
architecture.title: Architecture

cicd.sectionLabel: // ci/cd pipeline — "works on my machine" is not a done criterion

stack.sectionLabel: // stack in context — not a list, a decision
stack.title: Stack

note1.title: Why I abandoned Strapi and built my own stack
note1.summary: Multi-tenancy with RLS, feature flags and the decision that transformed a headless system into a real SaaS. What Strapi can't do when you scale to 200 branches.

note2.title: AGENTS.md and atomic tasks — AI as a tool, not a shortcut
note2.summary: How I structure context (versioned knowledge base, PRD, AGENTS.md) so the agent produces consistent architecture instead of random code.

note3.title: Transport → Application → Domain → Infra in practice
note3.summary: Real layer separation in a Fastify project with concrete examples from Diocese SaaS. What changes day-to-day when you respect boundaries.

footer.privacy: This website uses Microsoft Clarity to understand how visitors interact with the content and improve the experience.
